title: BARBIE
logline: When perfect Barbie begins experiencing existential problems in her fantastical world, she must journey to the real world to find the girl playing with her and restore balance to Barbie Land.
antagonistOrConflict: The clash between perfection and reality, externalized through Ken's patriarchal takeover and Barbie's existential crisis
tone: Satirical, whimsical, and ultimately heartfelt - balancing sharp social commentary with childlike wonder

actBreaks.description: Barbie and Ken arrive in the real world, leaving the safety of Barbie Land behind and entering unfamiliar territory.

validation.ruling: Scene #30 functions as a perfect Act 1 break where Barbie and Ken emerge from the police station into the real world, with Barbie committing to find the mysterious girl while Ken wanders off to discover patriarchy - irreversibly launching both characters into their Act 2 journeys of discovery.

actBreaks.description: Barbie returns to a transformed Barbie Land where Ken has established patriarchal control, forcing her to confront the consequences of her journey.

validation.ruling: Scene #69 serves as the Act 2 break where Barbie Margot hits rock bottom among the reject toys, learning she's immune to brainwashing due to her real-world exposure, while Weird Barbie declares Barbie Land will become Ken Land in 48 hours - the darkest moment before the final act's restoration effort.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script operates as a sophisticated circular journey that uses the fish-out-of-water structure as a vehicle for deeper existential exploration. The story begins in perfect Barbie Land, ventures into the chaotic real world, then returns to a transformed Barbie Land that must be restored - but the final restoration isn't about returning to the original perfection, it's about accepting complexity and growth. The structure cleverly mirrors the maturation process itself, moving from simplistic idealism through disillusionment to a more nuanced understanding of identity and purpose.

What makes this structure particularly effective is how it uses the real world as a mirror that reflects back the artificial constructs of Barbie Land, allowing both worlds to comment on each other. The script doesn't just complete a circle - it creates a spiral, where the return is to a similar but fundamentally changed place, much like how childhood toys take on different meanings when viewed through adult eyes.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing follows a carefully orchestrated rhythm that accelerates through the three acts. Act 1 establishes the perfect world with deliberate, almost hypnotic repetition (scenes 3-15), then jolts into crisis mode. The real world section (scenes 30-54) moves at breakneck speed with chase sequences and rapid discoveries. The return to Barbie Land (scenes 55-69) creates a false sense of familiarity before revealing the shocking transformation. The final act builds to an elaborate musical battle sequence (scenes 77-79) before slowing to intimate, philosophical conversations with Ruth (scene 81). This creates a satisfying deceleration that allows the themes to fully land.

incitingIncident.description: At the beach, Barbie Margot can't laugh naturally, her feet cramp and she topples over, discovering her feet are no longer arched and she feels embarrassed.

validation.ruling: Scene #18 shows Barbie lying in bed thinking about death, which is a symptom rather than the cause. Scene #22 is the true inciting incident where Barbie's thoughts of death and cellulite manifest physically at the beach party, shattering her perfect world as other Barbies recoil in horror and she realizes something is fundamentally wrong with her.

midpoint.description: Barbie Margot enters a 1950s-style room where a woman dressed like a mom from the 1950s offers her tea, which Barbie successfully drinks despite some spilling down her chin.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. MAGICAL ROOM FROM THE 1950S. ETERNITY

validation.ruling: Scene #51 is a classic midpoint where Barbie meets Ruth Handler (the 1950s woman) in a magical kitchen space, representing a crucial point of no return where Barbie gains perspective on her identity crisis and feels 'just right' as she is, marking her shift from reactive fleeing to proactive self-acceptance.

climax.description: The Kens arrive at the cul-de-sac to find the Barbies have combined all aesthetics into beautiful Dream Houses, with Barbie Issa announcing they've restored the constitution and Ken Ryan Gosling realizing he has no one left to attack.

validation.ruling: Scene #79 is just a procedural vote, not the climactic confrontation. Scene #80 provides the true climax where Ken Ryan Gosling arrives expecting to battle but finds the Barbies have already restored their world, forcing him to confront that his entire patriarchal revolution has failed and he has no one left to fight - the ultimate resolution of the Ken/Barbie power struggle.

scenes.heading: EXT. A DESERT-LIKE-LANDSCAPE. DAY
scenes.summary: Little girls play with baby dolls in a desert landscape while Helen Mirren's narration explains how girls could only pretend to be mothers until something changed.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE SPACE
scenes.summary: Barbie stands in an empty film studio space as Helen Mirren narrates her evolution through the decades into every profession and type of woman.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE. BEDROOM. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot wakes up perfectly in her pink Dreamhouse and waves to other Barbies having their perfect mornings.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE. BATHROOM. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot takes a shower with no water, her hair looks amazing anyway, and she magically changes into a perfect outfit.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE. SLIDE. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie takes her slide down to the pool.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE. KITCHEN. DAY
scenes.summary: She eats a nothing breakfast and drinks a big glass of nothing.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot sails through the air from her house into her car while Helen Mirren explains how imagination works in play.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot drives through Barbie Land waving to other Barbies who hold every job, with only occasional Kens present.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE OVAL OFFICE. LIGHT PINK HOUSE. DAY
scenes.summary: President Barbie Issa signs a bill surrounded by Barbie Congresswomen and tells everyone to compliment each other.

scenes.heading: INT. NOBEL PRIZE THEATRE. DAY.
scenes.summary: Barbies win Nobel Prizes in Journalism and Literature while Barbie Margot cheers proudly from the audience.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPREME COURT. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie Sharon argues passionately before the Supreme Court about corporations and democracy while expressing her emotions.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. DAY.
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot drives past the airport as a Barbie pilot waves down from an airplane overhead.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE. DAY
scenes.summary: Astronaut Barbies float in space and wave down to Barbie Margot.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. BARBIE CAR.
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot waves up at the astronauts and salutes Barbie Mt. Rushmore.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. BEACH. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot arrives at the beach where Ken Ryan Gosling waits with a surfboard, and all the Barbies and Kens greet each other.

scenes.heading: EXT. / INT. AMBULANCE / HOSPITAL. MOMENTS LATER.
scenes.summary: Doctor Barbies tend to Ken Ryan Gosling's small scratch while he explains that his job isn't actually surfer or lifeguard.

scenes.heading: EXT. / INT. BARBIE MARGOT’S DREAMHOUSE & STREET. NIGHT.
scenes.summary: All the Barbies have a giant musical dance party with the Kens as dancing decoration, featuring Ken rivalry and jealousy.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE, BEDROOM. NIGHT. LATER.
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot tucks herself into bed saying goodnight and claiming she's not thinking about death, but her eyes fly open because she is.
scenes.turningPointNote: First crack in Barbie's perfect world - thoughts of death invade her consciousness

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE, BEDROOM. MORNING.
scenes.summary: Barbie wakes up groggy and stiff, makes a face at her bad breath, and feels that something is off.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE, BATHROOM. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie yelps at cold non-water in the shower, discovers her plastic waffle is burnt, and spits out expired milk that doesn't exist.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot tries to fly into her car but falls and belly flops, then pops up pretending she's fine while people stare.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. BEACH. DAY
scenes.summary: At the beach, Barbie Margot can't laugh naturally, her feet cramp and she topples over, discovering her feet are no longer arched and she feels embarrassed.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barbie's physical perfection breaks down and she experiences embarrassment for the first time

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE/CUL-DE-SAC. MORNING
scenes.summary: The Barbies throw Barbie Margot a send-off party while the Kens stand aside, with Ken Ryan Gosling and Ken Simu engaging in jealous rivalry.

scenes.heading: INT. CONVERTIBLE/EXT. BARBIE LAND OPEN ROAD. MORNING
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot drives singing loudly until Ken Ryan Gosling pops up in the backseat, causing her to scream and crash the car.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRANSPORTATION
scenes.summary: Barbie and Ken travel through various modes of transportation including speedboat, rocket ship, tandem bike, and camper van.

scenes.heading: EXT. VENICE BOARDWALK
scenes.summary: Barbie and Ken arrive on Venice Beach wearing rollerblades and getting strange looks as extremely good-looking aliens in the real world.
scenes.turningPointNote: First arrival in the real world - major location and reality shift

scenes.heading: INT. VENICE CENTRAL BOOKING. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot and Ken Ryan Gosling get their mug shots taken and are fingerprinted repeatedly because they have no fingerprints.

scenes.heading: EXT. VENICE BEACH STOREFRONT. DAY.
scenes.summary: Ken and Barbie exit wearing stolen denim cowboy outfits while security alarms go off and a guard chases them.

scenes.heading: INT. VENICE CENTRAL BOOKING. AGAIN. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie and Ken get fingerprinted again while the male cops leer and tell them to keep the stolen clothes.

scenes.heading: EXT. POLICE STATION. DAY
scenes.summary: Outside the police station, Barbie sits on a bench trying to clear her mind while Ken gets antsy and bored.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTURY CITY. DAY.
scenes.summary: Ken walks through Century City observing men in positions of power and authority, seeing businessmen, gyms, and a female secretary being dismissed.

scenes.heading: EXT. POLICE STATION. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie has visions of a girl growing up, playing with Barbies with her mom, then rejecting her mother and donating her Barbies to Goodwill.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barbie connects to her human - sees the rejection that caused her malfunction

scenes.heading: EXT. MATTEL HEADQUARTERS. DAY.
scenes.summary: The camera booms down the imposing Mattel headquarters building as a phone rings from somewhere.

scenes.heading: INT. MATTEL HEADQUARTERS LOWEST FLOOR. DAY.
scenes.summary: A Mattel employee answers the phone in a forest of cubicles on the lowest floor.

scenes.heading: INT. FBI HEADQUARTERS. DAY.
scenes.summary: FBI agent Dan tells Aaron at Mattel that two genital-less dolls answering to Barbie and Ken are loose in Santa Monica.

scenes.heading: INT. MATTEL HEADQUARTERS. ELEVATOR.
scenes.summary: Aaron takes an elevator up through floors 99, 100, 101 and walks briskly down a huge hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. MATTEL HEADQUARTERS. BOARDROOM RECEPTION.
scenes.summary: Gloria sits at a reception desk drawing sketches of a distressed Barbie with mascara running down her cheeks.

scenes.heading: INT. MATTEL HEADQUARTERS. BOARDROOM
scenes.summary: Aaron enters a pink glittery boardroom where the CEO and male executives discuss selling dreams and female agency until Aaron interrupts.

scenes.heading: EXT. JUNIOR HIGH SCHOOL. AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Barbie and Ken approach Davey Crocket Junior High School where Barbie explains she's started having anxiety.

scenes.heading: EXT. JUNIOR HIGH SCHOOL. CAFETERIA
scenes.summary: Barbie spots her girl Sasha at a popular table in the cafeteria and approaches despite warnings, only to be brutally insulted and called a fascist.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barbie faces harsh rejection and criticism from the girl she's meant to help

scenes.heading: EXT. JUNIOR HIGH SCHOOL.
scenes.summary: Ken runs out of the library with books about patriarchy and is stunned when a woman asks him for the time respectfully.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE BUILDING
scenes.summary: Ken tries to get a high-level job but is told he needs education, and an office employee hints that patriarchy still exists but is hidden better now.

scenes.heading: INT. DOCTORS OFFICE
scenes.summary: A female doctor refuses to let Ken perform surgery just for being a man and won't get him coffee.

scenes.heading: EXT. VENICE BEACH
scenes.summary: Ken applies to be "Beach" at Venice Beach but confuses the lifeguard by saying he's only trained to stand confidently on sand.

scenes.heading: EXT. JUNIOR HIGH SCHOOL
scenes.summary: Barbie sits weeping in the school parking lot while Ken returns defeated, then Mattel agents in dark suits approach and order Barbie to come with them.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barbie is captured by Mattel agents - major plot development

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. BLACK VAN. DAY
scenes.summary: In the speeding van, Barbie chatters cheerfully to the silent Mattel agents, assuming they wanted her to come to the real world.

scenes.heading: EXT. GIANT MATTEL HEADQUARTERS. DAY
scenes.summary: The black van arrives at giant Mattel headquarters and Barbie steps out in awe, calling it the Mothership.

scenes.heading: INT. MATTEL HEADQUARTERS. LOBBY
scenes.summary: Barbie is escorted through the lobby and up a glass elevator to the top floor of pink executive offices.

scenes.heading: INT. MATTEL HEADQUARTERS. BOARDROOM.
scenes.summary: The Mattel executives greet Barbie enthusiastically, she pours mineral water all over herself, then they reveal a human-sized Barbie box they want her to get into.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mattel's true plan revealed - they want to box Barbie up, forcing her to choose between compliance and freedom

scenes.heading: INT. MATTEL CUBICLES. CONTINUOUS.
scenes.summary: Barbie flees through Mattel's cubicle maze while the CEO and executives chase her, with the CEO falling over cubicles as she sprints toward the exit.

scenes.heading: INT. MAGICAL ROOM FROM THE 1950S. ETERNITY
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot enters a 1950s-style room where a woman dressed like a mom from the 1950s offers her tea, which Barbie successfully drinks despite some spilling down her chin.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barbie meets a mysterious guide figure who offers wisdom and sanctuary

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY/EXT. STREET
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot runs from Mattel executives across a lobby to the street where Gloria's car arrives and she leaps in as the executives chase them.

scenes.heading: INT. GLORIA’S CAR.
scenes.summary: Gloria drives frantically while being chased by Mattel executives as she explains to Barbie and Sasha how her lonely drawings of Barbies became sad and weird.

scenes.heading: EXT. VENICE BEACH. 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Gloria, Sasha, and Barbie roller blade toward Barbie Land while Gloria explains she never gets to do anything adventurous, and they leave behind Nerdy Well Meaning Dad who practices Spanish.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. BARBIE CAR. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie drives Gloria and Sasha through Barbie Land while singing, but notices strange changes as Kens play volleyball and race on the beach instead of Barbies.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. DAY.
scenes.summary: Gloria marvels at the transparent Dreamhouses as they drive through the neighborhood, but Barbie notices more strange elements like an unfamiliar Hummer and masculine accessories everywhere.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE MARGOT’S DREAMHOUSE
scenes.summary: Barbie discovers Ken has transformed her Dreamhouse into his "Mojo Dojo Casa House" filled with masculine items, and he tells her she can stay as his bride-wife while other Barbies serve the Kens.

scenes.heading: EXT. VENICE BEACH. REAL WORLD. DAY
scenes.summary: Mattel executives roller blade while the CEO explains they must retrieve Gloria and close the portal or their world could be altered forever.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE MARGOT’S DREAMHOUSE
scenes.summary: Ken Ryan Gosling swings a golf club in the yard and offers Barbie the option to stay as his bride-wife at his Mojo Dojo Casa House, while brainwashed Barbies serve snacks to the Kens.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAPITOL STEPS. ON THE TV. DAY
scenes.summary: A newswoman interviews Ken Scott, now called Mr. Ken President Prime Minister Man, about the changes Kens have made to Barbie Land, while Ken Ryan Gosling accepts a Nobel Prize in Horses.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE KEN OVAL OFFICE. DAY
scenes.summary: Ken Scott signs a bill into law surrounded by Ken senators in the Ken Oval Office.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPREME COURT. DAY
scenes.summary: Ken Kingsley argues before the Ken Supreme Court while the gallery applauds.

scenes.heading: EXT. PINK HOUSE. DAY
scenes.summary: Ken Scott announces that in 48 hours all Kens will vote to change the constitution to a government for, of, and by the Kens.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOJO DOJO CASA HOUSE. SAME
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot confronts Ken about undoing everything the Barbies built, and Ken responds that now she knows how it feels, asking if it's fun.

scenes.heading: EXT. CUL-DE-SAC. DAY
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot runs screaming from the house as Ken throws all her clothes and accessories from the third floor, calling out each outfit by name before collapsing emotionally.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barbie loses her home and possessions, hitting rock bottom as Ken's takeover becomes complete

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE KEN LAND STREET. DAY.
scenes.summary: Weird Barbie rescues a catatonic Barbie Margot from the street in her makeshift tank-like vehicle while a Ken changes street signs from Barbie names to Ken names.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRANSPORTATION MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mattel executives ride a 12-person tandem bike toward Barbie Land with the CEO enthusiastically leading the group.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. BARBIE CAR. OPEN ROAD.
scenes.summary: Gloria and Sasha drive and sing together until Ken takes over the radio station, then Allan suddenly appears in the backseat causing them to scream and crash, landing safely beside their car.

scenes.heading: INT. WEIRD BARBIE'S WEIRDHOUSE. DAY
scenes.summary: At Weird Barbie's house, the group attempts to un-brainwash other Barbies while Barbie Margot lies motionless on the floor, and Weird Barbie questions why the brainwashing didn't work on her.
scenes.turningPointNote: The resistance forms and begins planning to save Barbie Land, with Barbie Margot's immunity to brainwashing becoming key

scenes.heading: INT. WEIRD BARBIE’S WEIRDHOUSE
scenes.summary: The now-unbrainwashed Barbies plan to turn the Kens against each other, while Gloria applies makeup to Barbie Margot who worries about hurting Ken despite everything he's done.

scenes.heading: EXT. KEN’S MOJO DOJO CASA HOUSE
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot visits Ken at his house where he pretends to be reading and they awkwardly discuss the new names for their land.

scenes.heading: INT. KEN’S MOJO DOJO CASA HOUSE
scenes.summary: Ken Ryan Gosling plays guitar and sings "Push" to Barbie Margot while she listens patiently on the couch.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. BEACH. NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a beach party four hours later, all the Kens play guitar to their Barbies until Barbie Margot pretends to text another Ken, causing Ken Ryan Gosling to become instantly insecure and jealous.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOJO DOJO CASA HOUSE. DAWN
scenes.summary: Ken Ryan Gosling wakes up singing and leads other Kens toward battle in a scene reminiscent of "The Warriors."

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. BEACH. DAWN
scenes.summary: The Kens arrive at the beach on paddle boats and hobby-horses for battle, with Ken Ryan Gosling screaming unhinged threats while they play volleyball and build sand castles.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF OF WEIRD BARBIE’S. MORNING
scenes.summary: All the Barbies watch from Weird Barbie's roof as Gloria observes the Kens destroying themselves, and they decide to go restore their constitution.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. BEACH
scenes.summary: Mattel executives appear on the beach among the battling Kens, with one executive getting shot in the arm by what appears to be a real weapon.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE LAND. BEACH. INTERCUT
scenes.summary: The Ken battle transitions into a dream ballet expressing frustrated masculinity before Ken Kingsley reminds them they were supposed to vote today.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBIE SUPREME COURT. BARBIE/KEN LAND. DAY
scenes.summary: In the Supreme Court, Barbie Issa leads the Barbies in voting "aye" to restore Barbie Land's constitution while Sasha tearfully holds her mother's hand.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Barbies successfully vote to restore their constitution, reclaiming Barbie Land

scenes.heading: EXT. BARBIE DREAMHOUSE. DAY
scenes.summary: The Kens arrive at the cul-de-sac to find the Barbies have combined all aesthetics into beautiful Dream Houses, with Barbie Issa announcing they've restored the constitution and Ken Ryan Gosling realizing he has no one left to attack.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Kens' rebellion completely collapses as they find themselves with no enemy to fight

scenes.heading: INT. ORIGIN SPACE
scenes.summary: In a vast illuminated space, Ruth (the 1950s woman) counsels Barbie Margot about her future, and Barbie realizes she may not be Barbie anymore while Ruth explains that humans have only one ending.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barbie makes the decision to become human and leave Barbie Land forever

scenes.heading: EXT. REAL WORLD. LOS ANGELES. DAY
scenes.summary: In Los Angeles, Gloria's family drops off Barbie Margot who nervously thanks them before heading to her appointment.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE. CONTINUOUS.
scenes.summary: Barbie Margot approaches a reception desk, gives her name as Barbara Handler, and proudly announces she's there to see her gynecologist before the film cuts to black.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barbie's transformation to full humanity is complete as she embraces her biological reality
